author: Flours
text:
§12/§13 tracker plugin: remote sync/push, prime, trail pointer

Adds the tracker, balls' one remote-talker, as a separate in-repo binary invoked subprocess-uniform over the §7 wire (no return channel). It does three git acts on the state branch, all driven by the wire binding and all no-ops in a stealth (no-remote) repo: sync (sync/pre) fetches + fast-forward-only merges, where a non-ff IS the contention signal; push (*/post) publishes the sealed balls branch; prime (prime/pre) resolves the upstream (committed trail pointer wins over the wire remote), adopts an established remote or founds an absent one (bootstrap-on-miss), or writes the self-lock in stealth.

Scoped to the wire-determined behaviors: the full prime materialization (operating/ -> territory symlink, landing model) and the multi-hop trail WALK depend on core's unbuilt prime/sync orchestration, so the pointer module is read-only for now (bl prime --center writes it when that flag is plumbed). Reuses crate::layout/encoding; self-contained git runner kept apart from the §8 terminus seal (core stays local-only, remote-talk is the plugin's alone). 100% covered via fixture-repo unit tests + a binary integration test.
